title: Floral Fluffy Nostalgic Japanese Carrot Soup
description: A diabetic-friendly, gentle-flavored soup that is both nourishing and comforting, perfect for a nostalgic winter meal.

ingredients.name: 1 cup grated carrots
ingredients.notes: Use baby carrots or regular ones finely grated.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup unsweetened applesauce
ingredients.notes: Can replace all or part of the water for a milder flavor.

ingredients.name: 3 cups low-sodium chicken broth
ingredients.notes: Use boneless, skinless chicken breast for best results.

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup full-fat coconut milk
ingredients.notes: Can be substituted with almond or soy milk to reduce calorie intake.

instructions: Preheat a medium saucepan over medium heat.
Add the grated carrots and unsweetened applesauce to the saucepan. Stir occasionally until the vegetables are heated through, about 5 minutes.
Pour in the chicken broth, coconut milk, ginger, and cinnamon powder. Bring to a simmer. Reduce heat to low and allow it to gently simmer for 20-30 minutes.
Using an immersion blender or regular blender, puree some of the soup until smooth. This step is optional but recommended for a smoother texture.
Strain the blended mixture into another saucepan if needed to remove any larger chunks before serving.
Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper to taste, then serve immediately.
